questions: Identify the specific choir sample library recommended for beginners that features 'polyphonic true legato' and a children's choir, and is frequently highlighted as a budget-friendly option offering distinct SATB sections in contrast to Audio Imperia's 'Chorus'. Provide the name of the library and its developer. Then, based on its official documentation or product details, report: (1) The exact sampling software required to run it (specifically addressing whether the full paid version is necessary), (2) The specific function of the 'Velocity Dynamic Influencer' feature and how it interacts with the Modwheel, and (3) The names of the specific flagship libraries from this developer's 'Next Generation' series that serve as the source material or crossgrade options for this collection.

rounds.question: Identify the specific choir sample library recommended for beginners that features 'polyphonic true legato' and a children's choir, and is frequently highlighted as a budget-friendly option offering distinct SATB sections in contrast to Audio Imperia's 'Chorus'. Provide the name of the library and its developer. Then, based on its official documentation or product details, report: (1) The exact sampling software required to run it (specifically addressing whether the full paid version is necessary), (2) The specific function of the 'Velocity Dynamic Influencer' feature and how it interacts with the Modwheel, and (3) The names of the specific flagship libraries from this developer's 'Next Generation' series that serve as the source material or crossgrade options for this collection.
checklist_width: Sampler Requirement: Native Instruments Kontakt Player (Free version is sufficient/supported)
Velocity Dynamic Influencer Function: Determines the loudness of single chord notes based on velocity (allows highlighting specific notes)
Modwheel Function: Controls crossfading between natural dynamic layers (while velocity handles individual note loudness)
Source/Related Libraries: Storm Choir Ultimate (or Rhodope 2)
checklist_depth: Logic Proof: Matches description of beginner recommendation with polyphonic true legato, children's choir, and SATB sections compared against Audio Imperia's Chorus.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify 'Choir Essentials' by filtering through beginner recommendations and specific feature comparisons (SATB vs Chorus) found in the source text. It then requires Wide Aggregation to retrieve specific technical details (software compatibility, specific feature mechanics, and related product lineage) that are detailed in the product's specific documentation/store page.

rounds.answer_a:
**Choir Essentials by Strezov Sampling** is the specific choir sample library recommended for beginners. It features **polyphonic true legato**, a children's choir, and distinct SATB sections (soprano, alto, tenor, bass), while being frequently praised as a budget-friendly option under $150, contrasting Audio Imperia's more blended **Chorus**.[4][5][7][9]

(1) It requires **Native Instruments Kontakt 6.4.0 or higher**; the free Kontakt Player version is sufficient—no full paid **Kontakt** license is necessary.[4][5]

(2) The **Velocity Dynamic Influencer** feature crossfades dynamic layers based on note velocity for expressive control, layering with the **Modwheel** (CC1), which handles additional swells and vowel/formant shaping for smooth transitions without clashing.[4][7]

(3) The flagship **'Next Generation' series** libraries—**Arva NG**, **Adore NG**, and **Astra NG**—provide the source material and crossgrade discounts for Choir Essentials owners.[5][6]

This library offers fluid polyphonic legato playable via sustain pedal for chords and melodies, plus shorts, sustains, and kids' patches, making it ideal for beginners seeking affordability and playability.[4][7][9]

(198 words)

rounds.answer_b:
The specific choir sample library recommended for beginners, featuring polyphonic true legato and a children's choir, is "Choir Essentials" by Strezov Sampling. It is often praised as a budget-friendly option with distinct SATB (Soprano, Alto, Tenor, Bass) sections, providing more separation and clarity compared to the blended sound of Audio Imperia's "Chorus" library [1][2].

(1) Choir Essentials requires Native Instruments Kontakt, specifically version 6.0.2 or higher. It is fully compatible with the free Kontakt Player edition, meaning the full paid version of Kontakt is not necessary to run it. Users can load and use the library directly in the Player without additional purchases, though the full Kontakt offers advanced editing capabilities not required for basic operation [1][3].

(2) The Velocity Dynamic Influencer feature allows users to adjust how MIDI note velocity impacts the overall dynamic intensity and timbre of the choir samples, enabling more expressive performances. It interacts with the Modwheel (CC1) by blending velocity-based variations with the Modwheel's primary control over volume swells and crossfades between dynamic layers (pp to ff). For instance, setting a higher influencer value makes harder keystrokes trigger louder, more intense samples, while the Modwheel smoothly modulates transitions, enhancing realism in phrasing [1][4].

(3) The flagship libraries from Strezov Sampling's "Next Generation" series that serve as source material and crossgrade options for Choir Essentials are "Arva Children Choir," "Freyja Female Choir," and "Wotan Male Choir." These provide the core recordings, with Choir Essentials offering a streamlined subset; owners of the full versions can crossgrade at a discount [1][5].

(Word count: 274)

## References

[2] VI-Control forum discussions on choir libraries for beginners (https://vi-control.net/community/threads/).  
[3] Native Instruments Kontakt Player documentation (https://www.native-instruments.com/en/products/komplete/samplers/kontakt-7-player/).  
[4] Strezov Sampling user manual for Choir Essentials (available via product download).  
[5] Strezov Sampling Next Generation series overview (https://www.strezov-sampling.com/collections/next-generation).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly identified the entity and all sub-details, specifically the source libraries (Wotan, Freyja, Arva). Agent A hallucinated the source libraries, claiming the library was based on 'Adore NG' and 'Astra NG' (Adore is a much newer, unrelated library, and Astra does not exist in this context). Agent B also provided a more accurate explanation of the Velocity Dynamic Influencer feature.
